$SPCX is reportedly preparing a bond sale of at least $20B marking its first investment-grade U.S. dollar bond issuance.

Amazon says Trainium already supports $UBER, OpenAI and Anthropic which has driven more than $225B in revenue commitments and remains largely sold out.
$MU JUST HIT A NEW ALL-TIME HIGH

$ASTS is lighting up New York City to celebrate BlueBirds 8, 9 and 10 successfully reaching orbit.

The satellites are the largest commercial communications arrays ever deployed in LEO.

AI clusters now scale almost linearly with power and xAI’s Colossus 2 has already reached a full gigawatt.

If power scales roughly one-to-one with GPU count then every announced GPU cluster becomes a future demand signal for transformers, switchgear, cooling and generation.

Thats why the power picks-and-shovels names have unusually visible demand.

FUTURUM EQUITIES PODCAST EPISODE #46

Daniel and I will be live tomorrow at 1PM EST exclusively on the Futurum Equities YouTube channel covering: 

• $ASTS launching three satellites and the continued buildout of the space economy
• Jensen Huang breaking ground on $COHR’s InP laser fab and why optics could become next AI bottleneck for $AAOI, $VIAV, $MRVL, $CRDO, $LITE & $AEHR
• What the $SPCX Cursor deal says about the future of software stocks and workflow ownership
• $NVDA & $GOOGL moving to 800V DC power ahead of schedule as AI racks push toward megawatt scale and what that means for $NVTS & $VRT 
• Why Kevin Warsh’s first Fed meeting looked more like a hawkish regime shift than a dovish pause

We’ll leave the last 10 minutes for live Q&A so bring your questions!

$GOOGL launched Ask Ad Manager which is a Gemini-powered AI agent that helps publishers troubleshoot ads, analyze revenue and generate reports.

Google is pushing deeper into AI-powered ad operations with Yahoo already integrating Ad Manager into custom AI agents.

$QS signed a multi-year joint research agreement with Honda R&D to advance its solid-state battery platform and manufacturing processes.

Honda said QuantumScape’s technology showed “compelling and unique advantages” across applications including automotive.

$EOSE received its first purchase order under its 2 GWh capacity reservation agreement with Frontier Power USA.

The order supports Redbird, a 100 MW / 400 MWh ERCOT storage project using Eos’ Z3 technology.

$ONDS agreed to acquire Cyberhawk for ~$125M expanding into critical infrastructure intelligence across utilities, energy & industrial markets.

Cyberhawk brings 300+ customers, 95% recurring revenue, ~$95M of backlog & proprietary data from more than 500,000 asset inspections.

The Warsh Era has officially begun🏦

📺 THE SMARTEST TRADE TODAY MIGHT BE NO TRADE + $IREN TO FOLLOW $NBIS? & $SPCX ONE MORE PUSH?

After a volatile Fed-driven reversal today, my message is simple: stop trying to predict every market move and focus on controlling your process. 

Traders who got stopped out of longs and immediately flipped short risk getting caught on both sides of the tape. Sometimes preserving capital and staying patient is the right trade.

*

The market spent two days filling Monday's bullish gap, only to see futures rally sharply today. 

That's exactly why I cautioned against emotional trading. Markets often do the opposite of what traders expect after major events. 

Instead of chasing headlines or forecasting crashes, traders should focus on risk management, position sizing, and understanding why they're in a trade.

*

Technically, $SPY is opening around $746 with major resistance sitting in the $749-$751 area. The key question is whether buyers could reclaim the entire prior day's bearish candle or whether sellers would use the rally to sell into strength.

On $QQQ, futures are pointing to an opening near $733, directly between support around $729 and resistance near $737, leaving the market stuck in the middle of a range and requiring confirmation before taking aggressive positions.

*

Remember, not every market environment is ideal. 

Only a few months each year provide the kind of strong trends and continuity that allow you to aggressively press winning positions. 

During choppier periods, expectations should be lowered and selectivity should increase.

*

Among individual names, $NBIS continued to stand out as one of the strongest AI and data-center leaders, making new highs and holding key moving averages even as the market sold off. 

I now see $IREN as an interesting setup. After spending months consolidating inside a long-term channel, the stock appears to be building toward a potential breakout.

I bought call options and am watching the $63 level closely, believing that a move above it could trigger additional upside.

*

$SPCX was an awesome three-day trade for us.

After trading it from the $170s through $190 and eventually reaching as high as $225, I sold my position at $211.

While I don't believe #SPCX is finished, I think the stock now needs time to consolidate. 

Support near $187 becomes important as traders look for the next setup rather than chasing an already extended move.

*

So, the smartest trade now may simply be no trade at all.

Protect capital, avoid revenge trading, and watch whether $SPY and $QQQ can hold their gap-up strength.

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet
